title: City council clears the way for major housing development near Syracuse zoo
summary: Syracuse Common Council unanimously approved a $134M mixed-income housing development on 23 acres near the Rosamond Gifford Zoo. The Albanese Organization will develop 260 affordable apartments (50-80% AMI) with Home HeadQuarters building 27 townhomes, with future phases including 100 market-rate and 100 senior affordable units. The city is providing $5.2M in land sale proceeds, $11M in low-interest loans, and $18.8M in bonding for infrastructure, with state tax credits and financing expected to close in December 2025.

entity_refs.name: New York State Division of Housing and Community Renewal

political_signal.description: Mayor Walsh administration actively championed this deal as critical infrastructure investment; unanimous council vote signals broad political consensus on affordable housing as strategic priority.
